name: react-testing
description: Testing patterns for React with Jest and React Testing Library. Use when writing tests, mocking modules, testing Zustand stores, or debugging test failures in React web applications.
qualityReason: Specific testing library and pattern focus
capabilities: Write unit tests for React components with React Testing Library
Test Zustand store state management and async actions
Mock API calls and module imports in tests
Test custom React hooks with renderHook
Handle async testing patterns with waitFor and findBy
Set up test providers for React Query and routing
author: CJHarmath
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown content with TypeScript testing pattern examples. Static findings are false positives - the scanner misinterpreted markdown code formatting and metadata fields as security issues. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or command execution capabilities. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Testing React components requires understanding component rendering, user interactions, and async state management. This skill provides ready-to-use patterns for Jest and React Testing Library that help you write reliable tests for components, stores, hooks, and async operations.
userTitle: Write React Tests with Jest and Testing Library
limitations: Does not execute tests - provides patterns only
Does not configure Jest or testing environment
Does not generate test files automatically
Does not run or debug tests in real-time

useCases.title: Learn component testing basics
useCases.description: Get started with React Testing Library by following patterns for rendering components, finding elements, and verifying behavior.

useCases.title: Test complex state management
useCases.description: Learn how to test Zustand stores, handle async store operations, and reset store state between tests to avoid flaky tests.

useCases.title: Mock APIs and test async flows
useCases.description: Set up proper mocking for fetch calls, test React Query integration, and handle loading and error states in components.

promptTemplates.title: Basic component test
promptTemplates.scenario: Write a test for a button component
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Jest test for a Button component using React Testing Library. The component should accept label and onClick props. Use userEvent for the click interaction.

promptTemplates.title: Store reset pattern
promptTemplates.scenario: Fix store state bleeding in tests
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to properly reset Zustand store state between tests to prevent state from leaking between test cases.

promptTemplates.title: Async hook test
promptTemplates.scenario: Test custom hook with loading state
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a test for a useUserData custom hook that fetches user data. Include loading state verification and proper cleanup between tests.

promptTemplates.title: Router integration
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I test a React Router component that displays user details from URL params using MemoryRouter and waitFor assertions?

bestPractices: Use getByRole and findBy queries instead of test IDs for accessible element finding
Wrap async operations in act() to avoid state update warnings
Reset store state in beforeEach to prevent test pollution
antiPatterns: Using implementation details like class names instead of accessible roles
Skipping act() wrappers for async state updates
Testing internal state instead of rendered output

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for writing and debugging React tests.

faq.question: What testing frameworks are covered?
faq.answer: The skill covers Jest and React Testing Library patterns for component, store, and hook testing.

faq.question: How do I test components with React Query?
faq.answer: Create a test QueryClient with disabled retry, wrap components in QueryClientProvider, and use waitFor for async assertions.

faq.question: Is my test data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only provides patterns. Tests run locally in your environment with your test data.

faq.question: Why do I get act() warnings?
faq.answer: State updates happening outside act() cause warnings. Wrap async operations in act() or use findBy queries which include built-in waiting.

faq.question: How is this different from Jest docs?
faq.answer: This skill provides ready-to-use patterns for common React scenarios including stores, hooks, providers, and async flows.
